_spawn_isolation_note: Loop body inline in main (Opus); Step-3 reviewer + HALT challenger spawned as independent subagents.
verdict: Good app, but not top-tier yet
verdict_explanation: The filter extraction (now visible) lifts architecture, simplicity, test strategy and credibility. This loop resolves the duplicated render blocks (F-005) by extracting two pure, tested format helpers, which is also the render-dedup slice of F-006. What remains of F-006 is the non-duplicated per-board detail render still inlined in search_boards.

architecture_quality.proof: filter decision now a pure tested Module (_evaluate_filters, commit 18fa3f8); search_boards owns transport + orchestration + the remaining inline detail render. Residual: per-board detail render still inlined (soundboard-snag.py:1350-1390)

state_management.proof: single-threaded; one writer per concern; immutable instance attrs

domain_modeling.proof: BoardResult + ParsedBoard; sounds_info deliberately a plain (id,title) 2-tuple (guardrail: don't force types)

data_flow.proof: named-field contracts; residual: main re-filters results (soundboard-snag.py:1582) although search_boards already returns only has_downloads boards (a redundant filter)

framework_idioms.proof: idiomatic stdlib; defensive sanitization; HTTPS; no secrets

concurrency.proof: no async/threading — synchronous CLI, no shared-mutable hazard
concurrency.residual_blocking_10: time.sleep pacing is intentional server etiquette, not a determinism hazard
concurrency.residual_rationale_or_backlog_ref: Tool is deliberately synchronous; no concurrency model to make safer. Permanent design carve-out.

simplicity.proof: filter extraction shrank search_boards to ~678 (commit 18fa3f8). Residual at loop start: two duplicated render blocks (date-display + skipped-breakdown), addressed this loop

test_strategy.proof: EvaluateFiltersTests added (commit 18fa3f8) — filter decision fully branch-tested; 34 tests. Residual: rendering untested at loop start (addressed this loop)

credibility.proof: parse + filter both now test-backed (commit 18fa3f8); two named domain records; consistent behavior-preserving extraction history

strengths: Four behavior-preserving, independently-reviewed extractions (BoardResult, _parse_board_html, _evaluate_filters, render helpers) — a sustained honest refactor cadence
Pure helpers, parse, and filter decision are all fixture-tested at their real Interfaces
Synchronous design + defensive sanitization remain real strengths

findings.title: Date-display and skipped-breakdown rendering duplicated near-verbatim
findings.why_it_matters: Two copies of the same presentation logic must be kept in sync by hand; they will drift, and the duplication is untestable while inlined.
findings.what_is_wrong: The approx-updated date block and the skipped-buckets breakdown block each appeared twice with near-identical bodies (the only difference a 2-space indent on the date block).
findings.leverage_impact: A formatting change had to be made in two places.
findings.locality_impact: Drift between the two copies would produce inconsistent output for the same data.
findings.why_weakens_submission: Duplicate presentation logic is a simplification deduction and kept the formatting untestable.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Extract _format_updated_line(approx_updated, approx_source, stats) -> str and _format_skipped_breakdown(skipped_buckets) -> str (pure, no color); call from both sites, wrapping with the site-specific color/indent. Fixture-test both.

findings.title: search_boards still inlines the per-board detail render (~636 lines)
findings.why_it_matters: The remaining render section is sequential formatting that could be a tested helper, keeping search_boards larger than its orchestration role requires.
findings.what_is_wrong: After the parse, filter and render-dedup extractions, search_boards still inlines the per-board/final detail render (board name, URL, description, category, views, tags, sample files).
findings.leverage_impact: Maintainers still read the detail render inside the orchestration function.
findings.locality_impact: Low now — the render is non-duplicated sequential output; a change is localized but untested.
findings.why_weakens_submission: The last inlined render keeps architecture and simplicity a notch below top-tier; it is the final structural slice.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Next loop: extract a pure _render_board_lines(board, stats, include_dates) -> list[str] for the final results render, fixture-tested; the search loop just prints the lines. Stop there — do not extract the progress/diagnostic prints (they are I/O-status, not data formatting). No renderer class.

simplification_check.structurally_necessary: Extracting the two format helpers passes the Deletion test (the duplicated blocks vanish into one definition each) and makes formatting fixture-testable. Pure functions returning strings; callers keep color/indent.
simplification_check.helpful_simplification: search_boards shrinks; output formatting is now testable and single-sourced.
simplification_check.should_not_be_done: Do not move ANSI color into the helpers (callers own presentation context/indent); do not extract progress/diagnostic prints; no renderer class hierarchy.
simplification_check.tests_after_fix: test_soundboard_snag.py gains FormatUpdatedLineTests (known/with-source/with-stats, unknown, unknown+stats) and FormatSkippedBreakdownTests (empty, fixed-order non-zero-only) at the new helper Interfaces.

backlog.title: Extract pure render/format helpers for the duplicated date + skipped-breakdown blocks (F-005)
backlog.why_it_matters: Removes drift-prone duplication and makes formatting testable; render-dedup slice of F-006.

backlog.title: Extract the per-board detail render into a pure tested helper (F-006 residual)
backlog.why_it_matters: Final render slice; shrinks search_boards to an orchestration core.

deepening_candidates.source_friction_proven: Finding F-006 — the detail render is sequential formatting inlined in the orchestration function
deepening_candidates.why_shallow_or_misplaced: There is no render Interface for the detail block; it is inlined.
deepening_candidates.behavior_to_move_behind_interface: BoardResult-to-lines formatting for the final results listing (name, URL, desc, category, views, dates via _format_updated_line, tags, sample files).
deepening_candidates.test_surface_after_change: test_soundboard_snag.py asserts the rendered lines for a representative BoardResult without network.
deepening_candidates.smallest_first_step: Extract _render_board_lines(board, stats, include_dates) -> list[str]; print the lines in the loop.
deepening_candidates.what_not_to_do: Do not extract progress/diagnostic prints; no renderer class hierarchy or templating layer.

builder_notes.pattern: Extract formatting as string-returning helpers, keep presentation context at the call site
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: Duplicated print blocks differ only in color or indent.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Return the semantic text from a pure helper; let each caller add its own color/indent wrapper.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: _format_updated_line returns 'Updated: ...'; one caller prints it with a 2-space indent, the other without.

builder_notes.pattern: A fixed-order join helper beats repeated append blocks
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: Several `if count: parts.append(...)` lines repeated in two places.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Drive the parts from a (key, label) table in a pure function returning the joined string ('' when empty).
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: _format_skipped_breakdown iterates a labels table and joins non-zero buckets.

builder_notes.pattern: Know when a finding is nearly done and name the small residual honestly
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: After several slices, the remaining work is low-severity (non-duplicated sequential output).
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Downgrade the residual's severity to match reality and consider whether the last slice is worth a loop or is an accepted residual.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: F-006's remaining detail render is Noticeable, not Serious, once parse/filter/dedup are extracted.

narrative: Place — a good app, nearly through its structural backlog. This loop resolves the duplicated render blocks with two pure, tested format helpers, lifting simplicity and test strategy and single-sourcing the formatting. F-005 is resolved; F-006 is down to one low-severity slice (the non-duplicated per-board detail render). Ownership and concurrency stay trustworthy. The refactor has stayed subtractive and test-backed throughout, resisting the renderer-class temptation the rubric warns against.

loop_result.what_changed: Extracted two pure formatting helpers from search_boards: _format_updated_line(approx_updated, approx_source, stats) -> str and _format_skipped_breakdown(skipped_buckets) -> str. Folded the four duplicated render sites (two date-display, two skipped-breakdown) into single calls, with each caller keeping its own color/indent. Added FormatUpdatedLineTests (3) and FormatSkippedBreakdownTests (2).
loop_result.evidence_change_is_honest: python3 -m py_compile passes; python3 -m unittest test_soundboard_snag runs 39 tests, all OK (34 prior + 5 new); --help exits 0; grep confirms breakdown_parts no longer appears anywhere and the date-display literals exist only inside the single _format_updated_line definition; the helpers reproduce the exact strings (including the 'approx', ' via {source}', and '; track headers: ok/total' fragments) and the date block keeps its 2-space indent at the inline site; search_boards shrank ~678->636 lines.

implementation_review.reason: All three checks pass: helpers exist at module level, all four duplicated blocks replaced, behavior is byte-identical, callers keep their own color and indent, tests assert real strings, and no regression introduced.
